V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-05] [EPISTEMIC: 2026-04-07] FY2025 EBITDA ATH ($407M), but FY2026 guide is flat-to-+2% CeT, 32-34% EBITDA margin (down from 35%), CapEx ~$190M (nearly doubled), FCF conversion ~40%. CFO explicitly flagged take-rate compression in both PM (social/CTV mix) and RM (large retailer contract + display). This is stabilization, not inflection. **Earnings quality caveat:** FY2025 ATH margins partially flattered by non-recurring tailwinds: (1) server useful-life extension 5→6 years (Jan 2025) reduced depreciation [FACT: 10-Q Q3 2025 Note 1 p.9]; (2) Iponweb LUS roll-off reduced SBC $103M→$58M (-44%); (3) lower CPMs on purchased inventory reduced TAC [FACT: 10-Q Q3 2025 pp.27-28]. Normalized sustainable EBITDA may be $380-400M rather than $407M. Do not anchor on $407M as the repeatable baseline — 3 council agents independently flagged this. Management alignment decent (chairman $423K buying, $200M buyback) but not structurally locked. Score reduced from 4→3: council bear-case take-rate/CapEx warnings persuasive; bull-optionality prevented drop to 2/5.

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: Market prices CRTO at ~1.56x EV/EBITDA (~$637M EV on $407M EBITDA) — massive discount to 12-15x peer band. Price $18.50 vs council FV $50.5 = +173% upside; vs Claude FV $74.9 = +305%. Multi-method confirms: P/E at 15x peer-median = $40.8 (+120%); Owner Earnings cap @ 8% = $60.6 (+228%); trailing FCF yield 33%. Direction overwhelmingly clear. Score capped at 5 (not EXTREME) because vault documents conflicting EV/EBITDA figures (1.2x, 1.56x, 2.5x) and MGNI peer multiples (~6x, 18x, 22.2x) — valuation GAP is massive but precision is weaker.

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: All 5 drawdown causes classified TRANSIENT (see table). Mispricing reasons are specific and verifiable: (1) RM client losses misread as secular trend (actually 2 idiosyncratic clients, quantified headwind); (2) French domicile blocking institutional/index access (being addressed, 15-month timeline); (3) sub-$1B market cap triggering mandate-driven forced selling (Cadian -50%, Voya -100%, Lord Abbett -100%). Self-correcting mechanism: redomiciliation + Russell inclusion + buyback mechanically address barriers. **[2026-04-16]:** Prior 4/5 cap (RM-PM flywheel "credible structural alternative") removed — flywheel COUNTERED in DEBATES.md: PM CeT grew every year through RM headwind (FY2024-2025); largest lost client's data integration intact under multiyear contract; Shopper Graph DAU 685M→720M; OpenAI sole-partner selection validates data quality. All causes TRANSIENT with active remediation and dated self-correcting mechanism. (Sources: DEBATES.md 2026-04-16 counter; Q1-Q4 2025 Earnings Calls; 10-K FY2021/FY2025; Criteo PR Mar 2, 2026)

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: Net cash $389M on ~$940M market cap = ~41% of market cap backed by cash. Zero debt. ~$475M undrawn facilities. Total liquidity ~$891M. FY2025 FCF $210M. No covenant risk, no refinancing risk. BUT: council Model A thesis-failure probability 11-15% (permanent capital loss) and 35-45% dead-money/value-trap probability. Balance sheet is fortress but operating franchise can deteriorate without triggering distress. Score unchanged at 4.

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-05] Two top-level conditions: (1) $400M+ EBITDA durable; (2) multiple re-rates from ~1.4x to 3x+. But Condition 1 hides correlated sub-risks that bear-case agent clearly mapped: RM retailer-control pressure, PM targeting/take-rate quality, CapEx normalization, and unresolved data-rights dependency. Condition 2 depends on 15-month redomiciliation/index chain. Neither condition secure enough for 4/5. Score reduced from 4→3.

V6.name: Upside Optionality
V6.rationale: 8+ independent paths with early evidence: (1) RM headwind trough → growth reacceleration; (2) OpenAI ChatGPT ad scaling; (3) MCP adoption by other LLMs; (4) Russell index inclusion; (5) buyback retiring shares at trough; (6) M&A exit; (7) Commerce GO! self-service at 80%+ margin; (8) SA360 search-to-shelf budget unlock (600% ROAS, ~2 growth points guided) [COUNCIL: bull-optionality, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-04]; (9) auction-based display wallet-share expansion (49 retailers, 21% of on-site spend) [COUNCIL: bull-optionality, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-04]; (10) agency holdco standardization (38% of RM, 3/4 products) [COUNCIL: bull-optionality, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-04].

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: Core thesis rests on audited 10-K data (4 annual + 4 quarterly filings reviewed). 18 transcripts reviewed. EBITDA, OCF, balance sheet, CeT trajectory are all [FACT]-labeled from filings. Score reduced from 4→3 because critical gaps ARE thesis-critical: OpenAI revenue-share terms/exclusivity undisclosed, Shopper Graph data-source decomposition unanswered (key to RM-PM flywheel risk), FY2027 capex normalization unknown, MGNI peer-multiple unreconciled (affects V2 precision). These require resolution before upgrading.

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: 7 dated catalysts with estimated impact, clear sequencing. Key dates: Q1 2026 earnings (~May 2026), Luxembourg (Q3 2026), Russell (June 2027). All are specific and observable. Score 4 (not 5) because the full re-rating requires 15 months and 7 sequential events — timing uncertainty on US redomiciliation (must hit April 2027 rank day) could delay Russell by a full year.

blindspots: **RM-PM Negative Flywheel** — If RM clients in-house, does Shopper Graph data flow continue under technology-only arrangements? If not, PM targeting quality degrades, creating correlated revenue decline across both segments. QUESTIONS.md has open item on Shopper Graph data source decomposition. Key question: what % of Shopper Graph comes from RM integrations vs publisher-side vs first-party? [COUNCIL: bear-case, o3, 2026-04-04]

**Publisher Fee-Share Retaliation** — As TAC/revenue falls (benefit to CRTO), publishers may demand higher floor pricing or switch supply paths, reversing margin gains. Second-order effect not tracked by any current indicator. Monitor: TAC ratio stabilization or reversal in FY2026 filings. [COUNCIL: bear-case, o3 updated-model, 2026-04-04]

**Litigation Tail Risk** — GoodRx settlement DENIED by court (Jan 2026); AlmondNet patent pending; CNIL EUR 40M appeal pending Conseil d'Etat (ruling expected 2026-27). Aggregate worst-case $15-20M (above $7M GAAP accrual). Precedent risk: if tracking pixel class actions succeed on merits, Criteo's data methodology may require restructuring. [QUESTIONS.md Filing Review; COUNCIL: bear-case, o3 v2]

**Ad Tech Base Rate Gap** — No sector-specific case study in vault. Historical base rate for profitable ad tech at sub-2x EV/EBITDA re-rating within 24 months is undefined. Council estimates range 15-30% depending on adjustment factors. DEBATES.md Vector 1 (Base Rate Attack) remains OPEN. [DEBATES.md 2026-03-30]

**SA360 Search Budget Diversion** — First SA360 campaign delivered 600% ROAS [FACT: Q4-25 transcript p.7]. If SA360 channels meaningful search budgets ($170-200B global market) into Retail Media, this is a material NEW growth vector not in thesis V6. No indicator currently tracks SA360-sourced CeT. Monitor Q1/Q2 2026 earnings for any disclosure. [COUNCIL: bull-optionality, o3 updated-model, 2026-04-04]

**DoorDash/Delivery Platform Exposure** — DoorDash acquired Symbiosys ($175M) for in-house ad-tech. Uber Eats US exited. What % of RM CeT comes from delivery/marketplace platforms vs traditional retail? Not disclosed. Delivery platforms have stronger in-housing incentives than traditional retailers. [DEBATES.md Vector 2]

noiseFilter: **Daily stock price / sector correlation moves** — CRTO is sub-$1B, thinly traded (4 analysts), moves with ad tech sentiment. Daily moves are noise. Only act on sustained (>10%) multi-week divergence from fundamentals.

**Analyst ratings without updated financial models** — Initiation or reiteration at current targets without revised EBITDA/CeT estimates adds no information. Only act on estimate revisions with explicit methodology change.

**OpenAI headline noise (TTD/Amazon partnership rumors, "ChatGPT ads" articles)** — Market conflates "OpenAI adds partners" with "Criteo loses." Thesis framing brief clarifies: Smartly = creative optimization (different function), NOT commerce demand. Criteo = sole commerce demand partner as of April 2026. Only act on CONFIRMED competing commerce DSP integration announcement with live ad inventory.

**Cookie deprecation narrative resurgence** — Google officially abandoned deprecation July 2024. Any revival is recycled bearish framing. Retargeting ~40% of PM but Criteo proactively diversified. Safari/Firefox already blocked — that's priced. Only act on ACTUAL new browser policy changes.

**Single-quarter CeT miss in Q1 2026** — Q1 is the GUIDED TROUGH ($245-250M). Missing by $5-10M is noise within the guided range. Only a miss below $230M with negative H2 commentary would be structural.
**SBC volatility quarter-to-quarter** — Iponweb LUS roll-off complete. Quarterly SBC fluctuates with grant timing and stock price. Only track annualized run-rate vs normalized expectation (~$55-65M).

trendLog.assessment: Confirmed on track. CRTO onboarded as INVESTMENT 2026-03-31. INDICATORS.md created with full indicator set. All thresholds sourced from audited FY2025 data + Q4 2025 guidance. No threshold breaches. CapEx spike ($190M vs $101M) is GUIDED and EXPECTED within thesis — not a breach, but highest-priority structural indicator (#6). Next decisive data: Q1 2026 earnings (~May 2026).

trendLog.action: No breach — positive development. V6 (AI optionality) de-risked. Monitor Q1 2026 earnings (May 6) for pipeline/revenue commentary.
